acceptedAnswer.text: The Silicon Epoch is the decade in which artificial intelligence becomes the dominant force shaping science, work, geopolitics, and culture — driven by frontier compute, transformer architectures, and a new global power grid.

mainEntity.name: Which AI lab is leading in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of mid-2026 there is no single leader. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, xAI, DeepSeek, Meta, and Mistral each lead in different axes — frontier capability, coding, multimodal, open weights, or cost frontier.
Frontier-lab roadmaps converge on transformative AI between 2027 and 2030. The bottlenecks are no longer ideas but compute, energy, and post-training data — discussed in the AGI/ASI chapter.

The data wall is the projected exhaustion of high-quality human-written text for pre-training. Labs respond with synthetic data, multimodal corpora, RLAIF, and test-time scaling — covered in the Data Wall chapter.

mainEntity.name: Why is TSMC so important to AI?

acceptedAnswer.text: TSMC manufactures every frontier AI chip — NVIDIA Blackwell, Google TPU, Apple Silicon, AMD MI. Its 2nm N2 node entering volume production in Q4 2025 is the single biggest hardware unlock for the 2026 model generation.

mainEntity.name: Open weights or closed APIs — which should developers choose?

acceptedAnswer.text: Closed APIs (GPT-5.5, Claude Fable 5, Gemini 3.5) lead capability. Open weights (Llama, DeepSeek V4, Qwen 3.7, Kimi K2.5) win on cost, latency, privacy, and lock-in avoidance. Most production stacks now blend both.
